title: 第 64 節
en: 12. In (the Pragatha) brzhad indraya gayata (Rv.
1 68 AITAREYA-ARAiVYAKA.
VIII, 89, 1) (the word brzhat occurs). Verily, br/hat is mahat (great), and as endowed with mahat, the form of this day is perfect.

13. In (the Pragatha) naki/t sudaso ratham pary asa na riramad (Rv. VII, 32, 10) the words paryasa (he moved round) and na riramad (he did not enjoy) occur, and as endowed with the words paryasta and ranti the form of this day is perfect \ He recites all (these) Pragathas, in order to obtain all the days (of the sacrifice), all the Ukthas 2, all the Prz'sh^as -0-, all the 6astras 4, all the Pra-ugas5, and all the Savanas (libations).
Second Khanda0.

1. He recites the hymn, asat su me^arita/2 sabhivega/z (Rv. X, 27, 1), (and in it the word) satyadhvntam, the destroyer of truth. Verily, that day 1 Because the performance of the Mahavrata sacrifice moves the worshipper round to another world and gives him enjoyment. Comm. It is difficult to surpass the absurdity of these explanations. Na riramat means no one stopped the chariot of Sudas. But even if it meant that no one rejoiced through the chariot of Sudas, it would be difficult to see how the negative of enjoyment, mentioned in the hymn, could contribute to the perfection of a sacrifice which is to confer positive enjoyment on the worshipper.
The stotras following after the Ya^haya^nfya Saman, serving for the ukthya-kratus.
The stotras of the noon-libation, to be performed with the Rathantara, Brzhat, and other Samans.
4 The rastras, recitations, accompanying the oblations of a§ya.

The pra-ugas, a division of jastras, described above. 6 The type after which the IMarutvatiya-^astra is to be performed is the A^aturviOTsa day. Hitherto (from a tva ratham to nakl/i sudasa/z), all that is taken over from the type to the modification, i. e. the Marutvatiya, has been explained. Now follow the verses which are new and peculiar to the Marutvatiya of the Mahavrata.
I ARAiVYAKA, 2 ADHYAYA, 2 KUANDA, 8. 1 69 is truth, and as endowed with the word satya, truth, the form of this day is perfect1.
2. That hymn is composed by Vasukra. Verily, Vasukra is Brahman, and that day is Brahman. Thus he obtains Brahman by means of Brahman2.

3. Here they say: 'Why then is that Marutvatiya hymn completed by the hymn of Vasukra ?' Surely because no other J^ishi but Vasukra brought out a Marutvatiya hymn, or divided' it properly3. Therefore that Marutvatiya hymn is completed by the hymn of Vasukra.

4. That hymn, asat su me, is not definitely addressed to any deity, and is therefore supposed to be addressed to Pra^apati. Verily, Pra^apati is indefinite, and therefore the hymn serves to win Pra^apati.
5. Once in the hymn (Rv. X, 27, 22) he defines Indra (indraya sunvat) ; therefore it does not fall off from its form, as connected with Indra.
6. He recites the hymn (Rv. VI, 17, 1) piba somam abhi yam ugra tarda/^.
7. In the verse urvam gavyam mahi g?-z'//ana indra the word mahi, great, occurs. Endowed with the word mahat, the form of this day is perfect.

8. That hymn is composed by Bharadva^a, and Bharadva^a was he who knew most, who lived longest, and performed the greatest austerities among the 7?/shis, and by this hymn he drove away evil. Therefore if he recites the hymn of Bharadva^a, 1 The commentator endeavours to make the meaning more natural by taking in the word prahanta, he who kills the destroyer of truth. But considering the general character of these remarks, this is hardly necessary.
2 Cf. I, 1, 3, 3- 3 By separating the first tn'ka. from the second, and so forth.
I 70 AITAREYA-ARAiVYAKA.
then, after having driven away evil, he becomes learned, long-lived, and full of austerities.
9. He recites the hymn kaya mbha savayasa/^ sanila/^ (Rv. I, 165, 1).

10. In the verse a ^asate prati haryanty uktha. (Rv. I, 165, 4) the word uktha occurs. Verily, that day (the mahavrata) is uktha (hymn). Endowed with the word uktha, the form of this day becomes perfect.

11. That hymn is called Kayambhiya1. Verily, that hymn, which is called Kayambhiya, is mutual understanding and it is lasting. By means of it Indra, Agastya, and the Maruts came to a mutual understanding. Therefore, if he recites the Kaya.mbhiya hymn, it serves for mutual understanding.
12. The same hymn is also long life. Therefore, if the sacrificer is dear to the Hotrz, let him recite the Kaya.5ubhiya hymn for him.
13. He recites the hymn marutva;£ indra vr/shabo ra;zaya (Rv. Ill, 47, 1).
14. In it the words indra v/'/shabha (powerful) occur. Verily, powerful is a form of Indra2, this day belongs to Indra, and this is the perfect form of that day.
15. That hymn is composed by Vi^vamitra. Verily, Vi^vamitra was the friend (mitra) of all (vlsva).
16. Everybody is the friend of him who knows this, and for whom a Hotrz priest who knows this, recites this hymn.
zh: 12. 在（普拉加塔）brzhad indraya gayata（梨俱吠陀
參閱第一冊，第二章，第二節，第十四段。
168 愛達瑞耶亞‧阿蘭亞卡
VIII, 89, 1)（此處出現了 bŕhat 一詞）。確實，bŕhat 就是 mahat（偉大的），而且由於具有 mahat 的性質，這一天的形式才得以完美。

13. 在（普拉迦塔）中，「naki/t sudaso ratham pary asa na riramad」（梨俱吠陀 VII, 32, 10）一句出現了 paryasa（他迴轉）和 na riramad（他不享樂）等詞彙，而以 paryasta 和 ranti 這些詞彙所賦予的本日形式是完美的。他誦唸所有（這些）普拉迦塔，為了獲得祭祀的所有日次（days of the sacrifice）、所有烏克塔（Uktha）、所有普里什塔（Prish^a）、所有沙斯特拉（Sastra）、所有普拉烏迦（Pra-uga）以及所有蘇瓦納（Savana，獻酒禮）。
第二篇章

他誦讀讚歌《asat su me^arita/2 sabhivega/z》（梨俱吠陀X, 27, 1），其中的詞彙satyadhvntam意為「真理的摧毀者」。誠然，那一天¹ 因為大祭（Mahavrata）的舉行使禮拜者轉向另一個世界並給予他享樂。——註釋這些解釋的荒謬之處難以超越。Na riramat的意思是沒有人阻止蘇達斯的戰車。但即使它的意思是沒有人通過蘇達斯的戰車而喜樂，我們也很難看出讚歌中所提及的享樂之否定，如何能夠對一場應當賦予禮拜者肯定享樂的祭祀的完善有所貢獻。
梨俱吠陀頌歌（stotra）追隨雅吉亞尼亞薩曼（Yájñayánya Saman）之後，用於烏克塔祭儀（ukthya-kratu）。
正午奠酒儀式的讚歌（stotras），應與羅檀陀羅（Rathantara）、布里哈特（Brzhat）及其他沙摩吠陀（Saman）一併進行。
4 拉斯特拉（rastras）是伴隨索摩祭祀（§ya）奠祭的誦詞。

布拉烏伽斯（pra-ugas）是上述經典（jastras）的一個分類。６馬魯特瓦蒂亞祭式（Marutvatiya-^astra）應依循的儀式類型為阿虛圖爾維薩日（A^aturviOTsa day）。迄今為止（從特瓦拉坦（tva ratham）到那克里蘇達沙（nakl/i sudasa/z）），凡是從儀式類型轉移到其變形的內容，即馬魯特瓦蒂亞祭式的部分，均已詳加說明。現在轉向那些屬於大馬恆祭（Mahavrata）之馬魯特瓦蒂亞祭式新創且獨有的經文。
我·阿蘭雅卡（Āraṇyaka）第二阿第耶亞（adhyaya）第二匯第八頌，169 是真實，且如同被賦予了詞彙 satya（真實）的形態，這一天的形式就是完美的1。
2. 那首讚歌乃是由瓦蘇克拉所作。誠然，瓦蘇克拉即是梵天，而那一日亦是梵天。如是，他藉由梵天而得梵天2。
3. 在此他們說：「何以馬魯特瓦提耶（Marutvatiya）讚歌乃由瓦蘇克拉（Vasukra）的讚歌而完成？」誠然，蓋非瓦蘇克拉此聖者（Ṛishi）外，無他聖者曾作出馬魯特瓦提耶讚歌，或將其妥善分段³。因此，該馬魯特瓦提耶讚歌乃由瓦蘇克拉的讚歌而完成。
4. 那首名為「asat su me」的讚歌並未明確指向任何提婆，因此據信是向原人主（Prajāpati）祈願。誠然，原人主是無限的存在，因此這首讚歌得以贏得原人主。
5. 在一首讚歌中（Rv. X, 27, 22），他定義了因陀羅（indraya sunvat）；因此它不會因為與因陀羅相連而偏離其形態。
6. 他誦唸讚歌（Rv. VI, 17, 1）piba somam abhi yam ugra tarda/^。
在詩句 urvam gavyam mahi g?-z'//ana indra 中，詞彙 mahi（偉大）出現了。具備詞彙 mahat，這一天的形式是完美的。
8. 該讚歌由婆羅多瓦（Bharadvāja）所作，而婆羅多瓦是七位仙人（ṛṣhis）中知識最淵博、壽命最長、苦行最嚴苛者，他藉由此讚歌驅除邪惡。因此，若有人誦持婆羅多瓦的讚歌，1 評注家試圖藉由引入 prahanta（真理的摧毀者之殺手）一詞來使其意義更加自然。但考慮到這些論述的一般性質，這似乎並非必要。
2 參見第一卷，第一篇，第三章，第三節。 — 3 藉由將第一個三元組（tṛka）與第二個分開，依此類推。
I 70 梨俱吠陀‧愛多羅耶奧義書。
然後，在驅趕邪惡之後，他變得博學、長壽，且充滿苦行。
9. 他誦唸讚歌 kaya mbha savayasa/^ sanila/^（《梨俱吠陀》I, 165, 1）。
10. 在詩句 a ^asate prati haryanty uktha（梨俱吠陀 I, 165, 4）中，出現了 uktha 一詞。誠然，那一天（大祭禮 mahavrata）就是 uktha（讚歌）。被賦予 uktha 一詞，這一天的形式才得以圓滿。
11. 那首讚歌稱為《迦耶姆比耶》（Kayambhiya）。誠然，那首稱為《迦耶姆比耶》的讚歌，就是相互理解，而且是持久的。因藉此讚歌，因陀羅、阿迦斯蒂亞及摩魯陀眾神達成了相互理解。因此，若人誦唱《迦耶姆比耶》讚歌，即可用以達成相互理解。
12. 同一首讚歌亦為長壽。因此，若獻祭者為祭司（Hotṛ）所寵愛，讓他為其誦讀《迦耶頌歌》（Kaya-śubhiya hymn）。
13. 他誦唸馬魯特瓦讚歌；因陀羅薛刹波羅刹耶（梵：indra vr/shabo ra;zaya）（《梨俱吠陀》III, 47, 1）。
14. 其中出現了「因陀羅」(indra) 和「瓦夏巴」(v/'/shabha，強大的) 等詞語。誠然，「強大的」是因陀羅的一種形態，今日屬於因陀羅，而這就是那一日的完全形態。
15. 此讚歌乃毘濕婆密多羅（Viśvamitra）所作。實則，毘濕婆密多羅為眾生（vishva）之友（mitra）。
16. 凡是懂得此理者的朋友，以及為之而由懂得此理的郝特裡（Hotrz）祭司誦唸此讚歌者，皆為此人之友。
